new_targets.why: Second-highest star count in the x402 ecosystem after ClawRouter (12.5k★ vs 6.6k★). AI trading terminal for stocks, crypto, commodities. x402 topic, actively updated today. A merged PR here would be the highest-traffic proof point in the portfolio.
new_targets.gap: Autonomous financial agent with x402 payments but no agent identity layer. An agent placing trades needs to prove it holds FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED permission — Bolyra's permission bits map directly to Nofx's order tiers. No auth/identity in the repo.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra agent identity gate before x402 trade execution
new_targets.contact_hint: Check CONTRIBUTING.md and recent merged external PRs to gauge responsiveness before opening.

new_targets.why: 2.4k★ local-first AI agent with peer-to-peer skills economy and x402 payments. Active community. P2P agent-to-agent transactions need verifiable identity on both sides.
new_targets.gap: Agents trade skills peer-to-peer via x402 but there is no credential that proves an agent is a legitimate participant (vs a spoofed client). Bolyra's handshake proof adds cryptographic identity to both buyer and seller agents in the skills marketplace.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/example: add Bolyra mutual agent authentication for P2P skill transactions

new_targets.why: 624★ autonomous USDC-spending AI agent, Apache-2.0, TypeScript, same org as ClawRouter (BlockRunAI). Very active (updated today). Wallet-native agent that spends real money — highest-stakes use case for agent identity enforcement.
new_targets.gap: Franklin autonomously spends USDC via x402 with no authorization check on the calling identity. If the agent key is compromised, there is no revocation or scope enforcement. Bolyra adds: verify agent credential + permission scope before any spend action, with on-chain nullifier for revocation.
new_targets.suggested_pr: feat: add Bolyra authorization check before wallet spend actions

new_targets.why: 611★ main Daydreams framework for building commerce agents (A2A + x402 + ERC-8004 + MCP). Week 1 identified lucid-agents (a sub-repo, 187★) — this is the higher-traffic parent framework. TypeScript, active.
new_targets.gap: Framework provides payment rails across multiple protocols but no identity layer on the agent initiating commerce. A Bolyra middleware adapter here covers all protocols at once — one PR, three payment stacks covered.
new_targets.suggested_pr: feat: add @bolyra/sdk context plugin for agent identity on daydreams commerce flows
new_targets.note: Separate from lucid-agents (week-1 target) — this is the core framework repo with 3x the stars.

new_targets.why: 475★ MCP server for live data (search, markets, crypto, X/Twitter) with pay-per-call x402 micropayments. Same BlockRunAI org — if ClawRouter PR succeeds this is a natural follow-on. High-fit: exactly the 'authorized agent calls MCP tool for money' pattern Bolyra solves.
new_targets.gap: Any agent that holds an x402 payment method can call this MCP server. No credential check on which agents are authorized to spend or what data they are scoped to fetch. Bolyra adds per-agent READ_DATA + FINANCIAL_SMALL permission enforcement as a middleware layer.
new_targets.suggested_pr: docs/middleware: add Bolyra agent identity middleware for per-caller x402 authorization

follow_ups_drafted.comment: Hey! Following up on this docs PR — it's been three weeks now with no activity. I want to respect your queue, so: if this isn't a fit (format, scope, or content), just let me know and I'll close it. If it's just low priority, I'm happy to wait — or rewrite it as a shorter README snippet if that's easier to review. Either way, no hard feelings! Just don't want to leave an open PR that's adding noise.

Hi! Checking in on this agent identity guide PR — it's been about three weeks. Totally understand if Stripe has internal processes for external docs contributions. If a different format (code example, SDK integration, or a separate example repo) would be a better fit, I'm open to starting fresh. And if this isn't moving forward for any reason, happy to close it with no hard feelings — your call!

Hey! Following up on this agent authorization guide after three weeks. The idea is solid for trading APIs — verifying an agent's ZKP credential before it touches brokerage endpoints is exactly the kind of safeguard that prevents unauthorized order placement. But I don't want to leave open PRs unattended. If this isn't a fit, let me know and I'll close it. If you'd prefer it as a shorter code snippet or a separate example repo, I can pivot. Thanks!

Hey! Following up on the agent authorization guide — three weeks open with no activity. Happy to close this if it's not a fit, or reformat it as a shorter README addition if that would be easier to review. The core idea (verifying an AI agent has the right permissions before it can place orders or mutate inventory) is something I think adds value to any commercial MCP server, but I don't want to clutter your issues. Just let me know!
